description: Facts about therapeutic modality selection for different target types and mechanisms

facts.context: For secreted protein deficiency diseases, the optimal modality is:
facts.truth: mRNA therapy or gene therapy to restore endogenous production
distractors: small molecule inhibitors to block residual activity
antisense oligonucleotides to further reduce expression
antibodies to neutralize the deficient protein

facts.context: For dominant-negative mutations, gene therapy should:
facts.truth: silence the mutant allele while preserving wild-type expression
distractors: amplify both mutant and wild-type allele expression
replace only the wild-type allele with additional copies
delete both alleles and provide exogenous protein

facts.context: Small molecule kinase inhibitors are preferred when:
facts.truth: the target has an ATP-binding pocket accessible to competitive inhibitors
distractors: the kinase is secreted into extracellular space
the kinase has no catalytic activity

facts.truth: extracellular targets like receptors or secreted proteins
reducing expression of overexpressed or toxic proteins

facts.context: CRISPR gene editing is most suitable when:
facts.truth: permanent correction of a genetic defect is needed
distractors: temporary knockdown for safety testing is required
the mutation cannot be precisely targeted

facts.context: mRNA therapeutics have advantages over gene therapy because:
facts.truth: they avoid genomic integration risks while enabling protein expression
distractors: they require viral vectors for delivery

facts.context: For ion channel loss-of-function, treatment options include:
facts.truth: potentiators or correctors (small molecules) or gene replacement
distractors: channel blockers to prevent residual activity
antibodies to the channel extracellular domain

facts.truth: cancer immunotherapy targeting tumor-specific mutated peptides
antibody targeting specificity with cytotoxic payload delivery
distractors: small molecule inhibition with gene silencing

facts.context: CAR-T cell therapy is most effective for:
facts.truth: hematological malignancies with surface antigen targets

facts.context: Exon skipping using antisense oligonucleotides treats:
facts.truth: frameshift mutations by restoring reading frame through exon exclusion
distractors: point mutations by correcting single nucleotides
gene duplications by removing extra copies

facts.context: Bispecific antibodies are advantageous because they:
facts.truth: engage two targets simultaneously enabling novel mechanisms
distractors: have twice the half-life of standard antibodies
penetrate cell membranes unlike standard antibodies

facts.context: Gene therapy using AAV vectors is limited by:
facts.truth: pre-existing immunity and packaging capacity constraints
distractors: inability to transduce any cell types

facts.truth: repeated infusions of recombinant enzyme with appropriate targeting
distractors: gene editing to restore enzyme function

facts.context: Small interfering RNA (siRNA) therapeutics work by:
facts.truth: inducing mRNA degradation through RISC complex for target knockdown
distractors: blocking ribosome binding to all mRNAs
